_doc: Catalog the evidence-test-author agent READS before designing, so it references an existing measurement tool rather than re-proposing one. The orchestrator owns this file: a new descriptor the agent emits is matched here at build time (identical -> reuse; same measurement diff threshold -> parameterize; genuinely new -> build + register). descriptor_key = source:compute:operand:unit (the closed vocab below).

tools.name: operand_enrichment
tools.measures: for each W/L gate offset, the fraction of a fuzzer's saved corpus whose byte == the target (W) vs decoy (L) value, as a cmplog-vs-naive log2 enrichment
tools.applies_to: fixed-offset VALUE gates — i2s literal substitution, both pro (enrich>0) and anti (deplete<0)
tools.scope_limits: skips structural/length gates (returns no_gate_signature); needs >=3 W and >=3 L seeds in DB; on-disk corpus target only

tools.name: joint_necessity
tools.measures: median seed size + structural-token (e.g. sfnt tag) count for the vpc-RESOLVING vs cmplog-BLOCKING vs value_profile-BLOCKING seed sets
tools.applies_to: joint-necessity ASSEMBLY-DEPTH subtype (vpc-needs-both; vpc builds a larger structure than I2S-only reaches)
tools.scope_limits: blind to the VALUE-PRECISION joint subtype (vpc ~ cmplog size); --tokens is target-specific (font tags here, SQL keywords for sqlite3)

tools.name: value_distance_reached
tools.measures: per arm, the MIN over the arm's seeds of (min over sliding windows of Hamming(window, target_operand)/width); numeric mode = magnitude gap for inequality gates. The winner (vp/vpc) lands CLOSE to the gate operand, losers (cmplog/naive) stall.
tools.applies_to: vp-gradient families (CMP_MAP climbs a Hamming gradient to a gate operand) — the 16 value_distance_reached hypotheses; also the joint VALUE-PRECISION subtype
tools.review_status: validated (anchor vpc reaches '<<' dist 0 vs cmp/naive 0.5; 4/4 local anchors winner_closer=true). /code-review DONE (fixes applied)
tools.scope_limits: operand value per branch from signature.operand_literal / agent params (target-specific); short operands (width<=2) chance-alignment prone (flagged via caveat); reads on-disk seeds so on-disk-corpus targets only; basin_escape/modality variants NOT covered (need a v2)

tools.name: depth_reach
tools.measures: runs winner-resolving vs loser-blocking seeds through the libafl-<target>-cov image, reads the branch line's per-seed-normalized execution count + blocked-side count from llvm-cov
tools.applies_to: ctx/ngram ITERATION-DEPTH subtype (winner corpus drives the branch deeper per seed)
tools.review_status: reviewed + fixes (deterministic seed order, on-disk filter for per-seed denom, robust count parse, tmp cleanup, timeout catch, line-count MAX). Validated iteration-depth ctx (7/20).
tools.scope_limits: BLIND to the ctx context-reach subtype (standard cov binary has no per-call-context counters) -> those stay inconclusive; Docker per-branch (~30-60s); needs the target's cov image

tools.name: detect_conjunction_gate
tools.measures: STATIC: from the llvm-cov branch report, whether the gate is a CONTIGUOUS MAGIC BYTE-SEQUENCE conjunction (>=2 short-circuit branch-points on the gate line + >=2 per-byte == clauses walking consecutive input positions, no range/||) vs a single multi-byte compare (cat-1) or a grammar/state conjunction
tools.applies_to: vp_incremental_conjunction_climb — the byte-by-byte conjunction gates (BOM/magic/keyword sequences: Fig-2a in[0..3] BOM, RAW&&NXT 'ANY'/'ID'). Separates them from vp_gradient_value_distance_closure (single compare).
tools.review_status: new 2026-06-25; deterministic; detects Fig-2a BOM (4 clauses, staircase) + 2 validated VP-P members mislabeled into cat-1
tools.scope_limits: needs a per_role_coverage dump (the host source); cannot see branches without a dump (e.g. unadmitted BOM-detect gates) unless pointed at a dump that contains their source via --line/--dump

tools.name: conjunction_match_depth
tools.measures: DYNAMIC: per arm, the deepest prefix of the magic sequence reached by the arm's seeds (0..N). winner (vp/vpc) climbs the conjunction one clause deeper than the loser (cmplog/naive), which stalls partway — the incremental-climb fingerprint that survives the short-circuit coverage leak (so these need not pass the >=8/8 all-or-nothing bar)
tools.applies_to: vp_incremental_conjunction_climb (pairs with detect_conjunction_gate). Rule: n_clauses>=2 AND winner_deeper AND winner_match_depth>=n_clauses-1 AND match_depth_gap>=1
tools.anchor_branch: libxml2/6663 ('ANY': vpc depth 2 vs cmplog depth 1) + libxml2/6621 ('ID': vpc 2 vs cmplog 1)
tools.review_status: new 2026-06-25; confirms both validated VP-P magic-sequence members; loser stalls at depth N-1 exactly as the motivation predicts
tools.scope_limits: needs resolving_seeds/blocking_seeds lineage for the deciding pair + on-disk queues; unadmitted gates (e.g. Fig-2a BOM 6336) have no bisected corpus -> skip until seed_bisect is run

data_realities: I2S is NOT tagged in mutation_op — the havoc mutator vocabulary is byte-identical across all 4 fuzzers. ANY descriptor of the form seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence on an 'I2S event' is INFEASIBLE. Do not propose it.

value_profile (CMP_MAP) is a FEEDBACK, not a mutator — it is invisible in mutation_op too. Its effect is measurable only via corpus admission / what the corpus reaches (size, tokens, value-distance), never via a lineage 'gradient event'.
joint / structural-assembly gates have different-SIZED winning vs blocking seeds, so byte-alignment (byte_freq_ratio) fails — use struct_size_ratio / token_count / value_distance_reached.
on-disk corpora exist ONLY for curl, harfbuzz, openthread, sqlite3 — descriptors needing corpus/seeds only score those targets.
seed-dependent tools need resolving_seeds + blocking_seeds in db/blockers.sqlite for the branch (the 286 reps have them).
